chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the Rheonics SRV-X3 inline process viscometer with Tri-Clamp connection, including its purpose, associated products, and information on availability and compatible Tri-Clamp sizes.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

SRV-X3: Inline Process Viscometer with Tri-Clamp

Modified on: Mon, 30 Dec, 2024 at 6:25 PM

What products are involved?
SRV - Inline Viscosity Meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)

What is the purpose of this article?
To give an overview of the SRV with Tri-Clamp connection (SRV-X3), installation guidelines, instructions, special considerations and available accessories. Tri-Clamp connection varies in size but this article is valid for all of them.

1. Introduction
SRV is Rheonics sensor for inline viscosity measurement (viscometer). Read more on SRV
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) here
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)
At Rheonics, the SRV with Tri-Clamp connections is typically on stock in different sizes. For some high sizes, less stock is available, so the lead-time for delivery can increase.

See all (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors) Tri-Clamp sizes available for Rheonics SRV inline viscometer. (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors)
See all (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors)
key_facts: The SRV-X3 is an inline process viscometer that uses a Tri-Clamp connection.

The purpose of this article is to provide an overview of the SRV with Tri-Clamp connection (SRV-X3), including installation guidelines, instructions, special considerations, and available accessories.
The information in this article is valid for all Tri-Clamp connection sizes, despite variations in size.
The SRV is a Rheonics sensor specifically designed for inline viscosity measurement (viscometer).
Rheonics typically maintains stock of SRV viscometers with Tri-Clamp connections in various sizes.
For some high Tri-Clamp sizes, less stock may be available, potentially increasing the lead-time for delivery.
Support articles are available providing information on Tri-Clamp sizes compatible with Rheonics SRV sensors.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides technical details, dimensions, and specifications for Rheonics SRV inline viscometers, focusing on their Tri-Clamp connection and hygienic versions.
chunks.extracted_text:
CAD models for Rheonics SRV inline viscometer with Tri-Clamp connection.
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors)
(https://rheonics.com/cad-type-sr-triclamp/)
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors)
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors)
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411921-tri-clamp-sizes-compatible-with-rheonics-sr-sensors)

A Tri-Clamp (TC) fitting allows for an easy process installation in pipes and tanks. It is also widely used in the food industry, and at any place where frequent cleaning and disassembly are required. A Tri-Clamp connection is composed of 3 main parts: the ferrules, a gasket and a clamp. Find out how to select the correct gasket here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000407485-how-to-select-the-correct-seal-ring-for-tri-clamp-adapters-). Pressure and temperature ratings vary for a Tri-Clamp connection, based on the type of clamp used.

Rheonics offers the SRV-HS-X3 as hygienic viscometer with 3-A and EHEDG certificates (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412134-ehedg-and-3-a) available.

SRV-X3-15T
SRV-HS-X3-15T

Units: mm [inch]

Figure 1: Dimensions of Standard and Hygienic SRV-X3 version.

Specifications

Sensor (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) Rheonics SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)
Order code SRV-X3
Connection type Tri-Clamp (TC)
Sizes available From DN25/30/40 - 1.5" - OD50.5 up*
key_facts: A Tri-Clamp (TC) fitting allows for an easy process installation in pipes and tanks.
Tri-Clamp fittings are widely used in the food industry and where frequent cleaning and disassembly are required.
A Tri-Clamp connection is composed of 3 main parts: the ferrules, a gasket, and a clamp.
Pressure and temperature ratings for a Tri-Clamp connection vary based on the type of clamp used.
Rheonics offers the SRV-HS-X3 as a hygienic viscometer with 3-A and EHEDG certificates available.
diagram_labels: Figure 1: Dimensions of Standard and Hygienic SRV-X3 version.

2. General Installation Guidelines
SR-sensors can be placed at any point in a process and at any orientation but is helpful to consider some terms that are usually involved in the sensor measurements.

2.1 Submersion
The viscometer SRV has a sensing element shown inside the red section in Figure 2. This section of the sensor probe should always be fully submerged in the fluid since SRV measures what is in contact with its sensing element. Incomplete submersion can lead to inaccurate or systematic errors in viscosity readings. This is a common issue with unfill pipes due to very low flow rate.
When there is a chance of incomplete pipe filling then consider installing the sensor in a flow cell or assembly which would ensure the section around the sensing element is full before fluid exits. This can be done by making the outlet higher than the inlet (Figure 3a and 3b). However here consideration should be given to create self-draining installs that minimizes the chances of sedimentation.
key_facts: Smaller Tri-Clamp connections are available for flush sensor versions.
It is important to check if the required Tri-Clamp norm and size meets the requirements for Rheonics SRV sensors.
SR-sensors can be placed at any point in a process and at any orientation.
The sensing element of the SRV viscometer must always be fully submerged in the fluid for accurate measurements.
Installing the sensor in a flow cell or assembly can ensure the sensing element is full before fluid exits, especially when there's a chance of incomplete pipe filling.
To promote full submersion, the outlet can be made higher than the inlet.
Self-draining installations should be considered to minimize the chances of sedimentation.
warnings_notes: Incomplete submersion can lead to inaccurate or systematic errors in viscosity readings.
Incomplete submersion is a common issue with unfill pipes due to very low flow rate.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides technical guidelines for installing Rheonics viscometers and density sensors, focusing on avoiding stagnation zones and explaining the orientation independence of SRV sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 3b: Flow cell to ensure full immersion of sensor.

2.2 Stagnation/dead zone
It is not a good practice to have dead or stagnations zones around the sensing area (Figure 3). A stagnation or dead zone is where a fluid transfer is not good and older fluid may not be fully displaced by newer fluid. This leads to incorrect measurements due to part or all of the measurement being influenced by stationary fluid that is no longer representative of the actual process fluid.
Consider that some ferrules may cause dead zones in the gaps created with installation. Rheonics recommends the use of short ferrules or its (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/WFT-15T.pdf) weldolet that eliminates possible dead zones for the SRV and SRD standard sensors.
A clearance of at least 5mm is recommended between the sensor tip and a pipe wall or any other obstruction.

2.3 Orientation independence
SRV has a symmetrical tip, making it possible to face a flowing fluid in any direction. Its shape enables the fluid to be in contact with the tip without creating recirculation zones.

Flow lines
Figure 4: SRV sensing tip.
key_facts: Stagnation or dead zones around the sensing area can lead to incorrect measurements because older fluid may not be fully displaced by newer fluid.
Rheonics recommends using short ferrules or a WFT-15T weldolet to eliminate possible dead zones for SRV and SRD standard sensors.
A minimum clearance of 5mm is recommended between the sensor tip and a pipe wall or any other obstruction.
The SRV sensor has a symmetrical tip, allowing it to be oriented in any direction relative to fluid flow.
The symmetrical shape of the SRV tip is designed to ensure fluid contact without creating recirculation zones.
diagram_labels: Figure 3b: Flow cell to ensure full immersion of sensor.
warnings_notes: It is not a good practice to have dead or stagnations zones around the sensing area (Figure 3).

A stagnation or dead zone is where a fluid transfer is not good and older fluid may not be fully displaced by newer fluid. This leads to incorrect measurements due to part or all of the measurement being influenced by stationary fluid that is no longer representative of the actual process fluid.
Consider that some ferrules may cause dead zones in the gaps created with installation.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides guidelines for installing Rheonics SRV sensors in different fluid flow conditions, specifically addressing Newtonian and non-Newtonian fluids, standpipe installations, and Tri-Clamp connections to ensure accurate measurements and avoid dead zones.
chunks.extracted_text:
2.4 Flow
For Newtonian fluids flow rate does not affect the viscosity, so SRV should measure the same values in static and moving states of a fluid. For non-Newtonian fluids, the flow rate does matter and viscosity readings may differ between static and moving conditions. The recommendation for processes with non-constant flow rates is to install the SRV in a section in the pipeline with the most consistent flow rate to have a steady viscosity value.
Flow rate is also relevant to ensure the full submersion of the SRV sensing element into the fluid. For a process with a low flow rate, pipes may not be full of fluid at all points, so SRV should then be placed in a section where the pipe is usually full of fluid (i.e. after a pump) or consider the use of a flow cell.
In all cases, the SRV's sensing element should be submerged in the fluid. It's best to avoid installations with long standpipes (i.e. using long ferrules or a long elbow tee), since that may lead to a bad fluid transfer resulting in measurements that do not reflect the true state of fluid or worse high noise measurements.
When installing in a standpipe, choose the appropriate insertion length of the sensor by selecting long insertion probes. This allows the sensing element to clear the stagnation zone and be in the fluid that is of interest for non-movement zone
e.g. deposits
Measurement zone
A: Insertion length
Stand pipe
Figure 5: Long insertion probes for long standpipes.
3. Process Connection installation instruction
3.1 Connection steps
A Tri-Clamp connection requires two ferrules or flanges, a seal and a clamp, as in the next figure. Remember that, a short ferrule port (e.g. Rheonics WFT-15T (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/wft-15t.pdf)) is needed to avoid dead zones around the sensing element.
key_facts: For Newtonian fluids, flow rate does not affect viscosity, so SRV measurements are consistent in static and moving states.
For non-Newtonian fluids, flow rate affects viscosity, and readings may differ between static and moving conditions.
When processing non-constant flow rates, the SRV should be installed in a pipeline section with the most consistent flow rate for steady viscosity values.
The SRV sensing element must be fully submerged in the fluid.
In low flow rate processes, the SRV should be placed where the pipe is typically full of fluid (e.g., after a pump) or a flow cell should be used.
Long standpipes (long ferrules or long elbow tees) should be avoided as they can lead to poor fluid transfer and inaccurate/noisy measurements.
When installing in a standpipe, select a sensor with an appropriate long insertion length to ensure the sensing element clears the stagnation zone and is in the fluid of interest.
A Tri-Clamp connection requires two ferrules or flanges, a seal, and a clamp.
A short ferrule port, like the Rheonics WFT-15T, is necessary to prevent dead zones around the sensing element.
The diagram illustrates a long insertion probe for long standpipes to position the measurement zone away from the non-movement zone/deposits.
diagram_labels: Figure 5: Long insertion probes for long standpipes.
warnings_notes: It's best to avoid installations with long standpipes (i.e. using long ferrules or a long elbow tee), since that may lead to a bad fluid transfer resulting in measurements that do not reflect the true state of fluid or worse high noise measurements.
A short ferrule port (e.g. Rheonics WFT-15T) is needed to avoid dead zones around the sensing element.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides detailed installation instructions for Rheonics Tri-Clamp connections, outlining general steps and specific guidelines for inline and in-tank sensor placements, along with considerations for different pipe sizes and probe types.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 6: Tri-Clamp connection components.

The general steps for a Tri-Clamp connection are:
i. Have a welded ferrule in the pipe or tank.
ii. Place the gasket properly into the seat of the ferrule.
iii. Place the sensor Tri-Clamp connection on the gasket and verify it is correctly aligned.
iv. Loosen the clamp so it can fit around the ferrule.
v. Place the clamp, verify ferrules are within the clamp's groove.
vi. Start tightening the clamp until a leak-proof connection is made.

Be careful not to over-tighten the clamp, this can damage the gasket and ferrules.

3.2 Specific Process Connection installation instructions

•   **In line**: For inline installations consider the sensing area (Figure 3) and a minimum clearance of the sensor tip to the pipe inner wall (5 mm). A perpendicular Tri-Clamp installation of the SRV is not possible for most 1", 1.5" or 2" pipes (for 2" it depends on the pipe norm). In those cases, the installation is recommended to be parallel or with a flow cell, check accessories FET-15T-XXX (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/fet-15t-15t/) and IFC-15T-SRV (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/ifc-15t-34n-srv/). For **bigger lines** the sensor can be mounted perpendicular to the flow, Rheonics recommends the **WFT-15T** (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/WFT-15T.pdf) ferrule for such cases.

•   **In tank**: Place the sensor at the **bottom, wall or lid** of the tank as long as the sensing element can be fully submerged in the fluid and dead zones are avoided. The **WFT-15T** (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/WFT-15T.pdf) ferrule is recommended for these installations. Usually, it is useful if the sensor is placed at a tank's height that has always fluid. If there is a hygienic requirement, the SRV TC can be ordered with a hygienic design, the SRV-HS-X3 as in Figure 1 (optional 3-A and EHEDG certification) and should be mounted with a hygienic adapter (e.g. flush welding flange like ISO 1127).

•   **Others**: Larger pipelines or tanks often need a longer probe (i.e. jacketed tanks or pipes), in those scenarios, the client should use a long insertion probe (FPC sensors) - Order code: **SRV-X5** (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000411102-srv-x5-long-insertion-probe).
key_facts: Tri-Clamp connections require a welded ferrule, a gasket, and proper alignment and tightening to form a leak-proof connection.
For inline installations, a minimum clearance of 5 mm is required between the sensor tip and the pipe inner wall.
Perpendicular Tri-Clamp installation of the SRV sensor is generally not possible for 1", 1.5", or 2" pipes due to pipe norms; parallel installation or use with a flow cell is recommended.
For larger lines, the sensor can be mounted perpendicular to the flow, with Rheonics recommending the WFT-15T ferrule.
For in-tank installations, the sensor should be placed at the bottom, wall, or lid of the tank, ensuring the sensing element is fully submerged and avoiding dead zones.
The WFT-15T ferrule is also recommended for in-tank installations.

If a hygienic requirement exists, the SRV TC sensor can be ordered with a hygienic design (SRV-HS-X3), optionally with 3-A and EHEDG certification, and must be mounted with a hygienic adapter such as a flush welding flange (e.g., ISO 1127).
For larger pipelines or tanks that require longer probes, a long insertion probe (FPC sensors) with order code SRV-X5 should be used.
warnings_notes: Be careful not to over-tighten the clamp, this can damage the gasket and ferrules.
A perpendicular Tri-Clamp installation of the SRV is not possible for most 1", 1.5" or 2" pipes (for 2" it depends on the pipe norm).

If there is a hygienic requirement, the SRV TC can be ordered with a hygienic design, the SRV-HS-X3 as in Figure 1 (optional 3-A and EHEDG certification) and should be mounted with a hygienic adapter (e.g. flush welding flange like ISO 1127).

Important: Avoid using adapters to install the SRV in a Tri-Clamp port when the probe has a different process connection e.g. threaded. This is the case for NPT to Tri-Clamp adapters, as shown in Figure 8. Rheonics SRV should be ordered with the correct process connection to directly mount it inline without the need for additional adapters that can affect the correct immersion of the sensing element.

4. Accessories available
4.1 Weldolet - WFT-15T
key_facts: Proper clearance (minimum 5 mm) for SRV and sufficient immersion of the sensing element are critical for correct installation.
Using adapters, especially NPT to Tri-Clamp adapters, to install SRV probes with different process connections in a Tri-Clamp port is not recommended.
Adapters can lead to insufficient immersion of the sensing element, potential plugging, and the creation of stagnant zones.
Rheonics SRV viscometers should be ordered with the correct process connection to allow direct inline mounting, avoiding the need for additional adapters.
The WFT-15T Weldolet is an available accessory.

Rheonics SRV should be ordered with the correct process connection to directly mount it inline without the need for additional adapters that can affect the correct immersion of the sensing element.

chunks.page_summary: This page details several Rheonics accessories, including a weldolet, an inline adapter (flow cell), and a Tri-Clamp elbow tee, describing their connections, compatible sensors, and suitable applications.
chunks.extracted_text:
Weldolet for 1.5” Tri-Clamp sensor connection that ensures maximum submersion of the sensing element.
Suitable for the SRV and SRD X3 1.5”. It can be used for process integration into Process lines, mixing vessels, reactors, etc. Read more about this on its accessories page (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/wft-15t/).

Figure 9: WFT-15T ferrule

4.2 Flow cells
a. IFC-15T-SRV
Inline adapter for 1.5” Tri-Clamp sensor with 3/4” NPT inlet and outlet ports. This accessory is recommended for DN5 up to DN25 pipes, ensuring optimal flow over the sensing element and minimal stagnation zones. Read more about this on its accessories page (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/ifc-15t-34n-srv/).

Figure 10: IFC-15T-SRV

b. FET-15T-XXX
Tri-Clamp elbow tee for 1.5” Tri-Clamp sensor with welding inlet and outlet ports of 1.5” size. Also available for other line sizes on request. Review its web accessory here (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/fet-15t-15t/).
key_facts: The WFT-15T weldolet ensures maximum submersion of the sensing element.
The WFT-15T weldolet is suitable for SRV and SRD X3 1.5” sensors.
The WFT-15T weldolet can be used for process integration into process lines, mixing vessels, and reactors.
The IFC-15T-SRV is an inline adapter.
The IFC-15T-SRV is recommended for DN5 up to DN25 pipes.
The IFC-15T-SRV ensures optimal flow over the sensing element and minimal stagnation zones.
The FET-15T-XXX is a Tri-Clamp elbow tee.
The FET-15T-XXX is available for other line sizes on request.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes and illustrates two types of Tri-Clamp accessories for Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors: an elbow tee and a tee spool piece, detailing their connections and available sizes.
chunks.extracted_text:
c. FET-15T-15T
Tri-Clamp elbow tee for 1.5” Tri-Clamp sensor (DN25/32/40) DIN11850 with same connection for inlet and outlet ports. Also available for other port sizes under request. Review its accessory page here
(https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/fet-15t-15t/).
d. FTP-15T-XXX
Tri-Clamp tee spool piece for perpendicular installation of the SRV with Tri-Clamp 1.5” process connection. Only available with welding inlet and outlet ports of 2”, 3”, 4” or 6” size. Review its accessory page here
(https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/ftp-15t-xxx/).
key_facts: The FET-15T-15T is a Tri-Clamp elbow tee designed for 1.5" Tri-Clamp sensors.
The FET-15T-15T elbow tee uses DN25/32/40 DIN11850 standards for its sensor connection.
The FET-15T-15T elbow tee is available with various port sizes upon request, beyond the standard inlet and outlet connections.
The FTP-15T-XXX is a Tri-Clamp tee spool piece for perpendicular installation of an SRV sensor.
The FTP-15T-XXX requires a Tri-Clamp 1.5" process connection for the SRV.
The FTP-15T-XXX is exclusively available with welding inlet and outlet ports in sizes 2", 3", 4", or 6".
